truth.papers.A1.title: Current state of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels
truth.papers.A1.paper_scope: This review covers the physical metallurgy, processing strategies, strengthening mechanisms and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels for automotive applications, and suggests future avenues.

prediction.papers.A1.title: Current state of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels
prediction.papers.A1.paper_scope: Critical review of physical metallurgy, processing strategies, strengthening mechanisms and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels for automotive structural applications, covering ferritic, duplex and austenitic alloy classes.

truth.alloys.alloy_ferritic.alloys_notes: Matrix is delta-ferrite; Al content above 7% leads to embrittlement due to ordering (K1, B2, DO3).
truth.alloys.alloy_ferritic_duplex.alloy_name: Ferrite based duplex low density steel
truth.alloys.alloy_ferritic_duplex.alloys_note: Hot-rolled microstructure: gamma + delta-ferrite, with delta-ferrite fraction >50%. Cold rolling and intercritical annealing produce TRIP/TWIP effects.
truth.alloys.alloy_austenitic_duplex.alloy_nam: Austenite based duplex low density steel
truth.alloys.alloy_austenitic_duplex.alloys_no: Hot-rolled: gamma + small fraction of delta-ferrite (<50%); often contains intragranular nano-sized kappa'-carbides and delta-ferrite stringers.
truth.alloys.alloy_austenitic.alloys_notes: Single-phase gamma after solution treatment; precipitation of kappa'-carbide when Al>7% and C>0.7%. Best properties in Al 7-10%, Mn 15-30%, C 0.7-1.2%.
truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn1.2c.alloys_notes: Used as baseline in various figures to compare the effect of Al addition.

prediction.alloys.alloy_austenitic.alloys_notes: Fully austenitic lightweight steel exhibiting microband induced plasticity (MBIP).
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe304mn8al12c.alloys_notes: Austenitic steel studied for dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR).
prediction.alloys.alloy_austenitic_duplex.alloys_no: Austenite based duplex steel (triplex) exhibiting shear band induced plasticity (SIP).
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn8al1c.alloys_notes: Age hardenable austenitic steel with kappa-prime precipitation.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al09c.alloys_notes: Austenitic steel used for aging and impact toughness studies.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe28mn5al1c.alloys_notes: Austenitic steel used for impact toughness comparisons.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn10al1c1si.alloys_note: Austenitic steel used for tensile and impact toughness studies.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe18mn10al09c5ni.alloys_not: Austenite based duplex steel strengthened by B2 intermetallic phase precipitation.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe26mn97al047c.alloys_notes: Austenite based duplex steel used for formability assessment.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe22mn10al08c.alloys_notes: Austenite based duplex steel used for formability assessment.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe20mn11al15c.alloys_notes: Highly alloyed austenitic steel exhibiting negative work hardening rate.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn1.2c.alloys_notes: Al-free high Mn TWIP baseline steel for comparison.

truth.processes.proc_ferritic.description: Ingot casting, homogenization at 1100-1250 °C, hot rolling, cold rolling, recrystallization annealing at 700-950 °C.
truth.processes.proc_ferritic.processes_notes: Grain refinement limited; texture control via cold rolling and annealing.
truth.processes.proc_ferritic_duplex.descripti: Hot rolling in (delta+gamma) region, cold rolling, intercritical annealing at 700-900 °C followed by fast cooling or austempering.
truth.processes.proc_ferritic_duplex.processes: Austempering used to stabilize austenite. Control of austenite grain size and stability is critical for TRIP/TWIP.
truth.processes.proc_austenitic_duplex.descrip: Hot rolling in single gamma or (gamma+delta) region, solution treatment 900-1100 °C, water quenching; optional aging at 450-700 °C.
truth.processes.proc_austenitic_duplex.process: Process variants to control kappa-carbide precipitation and delta-ferrite morphology.
truth.processes.proc_austenitic.description: Solution treatment at 900-1100 °C in single-phase gamma region, water quenching; optional aging at 450-700 °C for kappa' precipitation hardening, or cold rolling + recrystallization annealing.
truth.processes.proc_austenitic.processes_note: Age hardening from intragranular kappa'-carbides; overaging leads to grain boundary kappa* and loss of ductility.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn1.2c_ref.descriptio: Solution treatment at 1100 °C, water quenching (reference condition for TWIP).

prediction.processes.proc_austenitic.description: Solution treated at 1000°C for 60 min and water quenched; produces single austenite phase with SRO.
prediction.processes.proc_fe304mn8al12c_01.descript: Solution treated at 1100°C for 120 min and oil quenched; produces austenite with SRO.
prediction.processes.proc_austenitic_duplex.descrip: Solution treated at 1050°C for 25 min and water quenched; produces austenite plus ~8% delta-ferrite and nano-sized kappa-prime carbides.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn8al1c_01.descriptio: Aged at 550°C for approximately 10 h after solution treatment; produces peak hardness via kappa-prime precipitation.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn8al1c_01.processes_: Age hardening response studied for peak hardness.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_01.descripti: Water quenched from 1050°C and aged at 550°C for 16 h.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_01.processes: Aged condition for tensile and impact property studies.
prediction.processes.proc_fe28mn5al1c_01.descriptio: Solution treated at 1050°C for 75 min and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_fe28mn5al1c_01.processes_: Solution treated condition for impact toughness testing.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn10al1c1si_01.descri: Solution treated at 1050°C for 1 h and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_fe18mn10al09c5ni_01.descr: Cold rolled to 70% thickness reduction and annealed at 900°C for 2 min.
prediction.processes.proc_fe18mn10al09c5ni_01.proce: Thermomechanical processing to produce austenite plus B2 stringer bands and nano-B2 precipitates.
prediction.processes.proc_ferritic.description: Cold rolled and recrystallization annealed at 700-950°C.
prediction.processes.proc_ferritic.processes_notes: Standard processing for ferritic Fe-Al steels to control grain size and texture.
prediction.processes.proc_ferritic_duplex.descripti: Annealed at 900°C for 30 min and water quenched; produces bimodal structure.
prediction.processes.proc_ferritic_duplex.processes: Intercritical annealing to obtain TRIP/TWIP effects from retained austenite.
prediction.processes.proc_fe26mn97al047c_830.descri: Annealed at 830°C for 1 min and fast cooled.
prediction.processes.proc_fe26mn97al047c_900.descri: Annealed at 900°C for 1 min and fast cooled.
prediction.processes.proc_fe22mn10al08c_01.descript: Annealed at 850°C for 3 min and fast cooled.
prediction.processes.proc_fe20mn11al15c_01.processe: Water quenched condition showing negative work hardening rate.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn1.2c_ref.descriptio: Solution treated at 1100°C for 2 h and water quenched.

truth.processing_steps.sample_ferritic.process: Grain size 40-90 µm after annealing; texture random with Cube fibre.
Controls volume fraction, grain size and stability of austenite.
truth.processing_steps.sample_austenitic_duple: Precipitation of intragranular kappa'-carbides; longer times cause kappa* and alpha (B2/DO3).
truth.processing_steps.sample_austenitic.proce: For non-age hardenable grades; grain refinement and ductility restoration.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.p: Peak hardness condition approximated from aging curves.

truth.structures.sample_ferritic.overall_struc: Elongated delta-ferrite bands with possible grain boundary kappa-carbides
truth.structures.sample_ferritic_duplex.overal: Banded mixture of coarse delta-ferrite and refined austenite (or transformation products)
truth.structures.sample_austenitic_duplex.over: Austenite matrix with delta-ferrite stringers and intragranular kappa' carbides
truth.structures.sample_austenitic.overall_str: Single-phase austenite with varying density of SRO / kappa' precipitates depending on aging
truth.structures.sample_austenitic.microstruct: depending on Al and C content

prediction.structures.sample_austenitic.overall_str: Single phase austenite with short-range order (SRO)
prediction.structures.sample_fe304mn8al12c_01.overa: Single phase austenite with short-range order
prediction.structures.sample_austenitic_duplex.over: Duplex austenite plus delta-ferrite with intragranular kappa-prime carbides
prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.overall: Austenite with nano-sized intragranular kappa-prime carbides
prediction.structures.sample_fe18mn10al09c5ni_01.ov: Austenite matrix with B2 stringer bands and nano-B2 precipitates
prediction.structures.sample_fe18mn10al09c5ni_01.mi: along grain boundaries and shear bands
prediction.structures.sample_ferritic_duplex.overal: Bimodal ferrite matrix with clustered austenite particles

truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic_duplex.phas: incoherent or semicoherent, crack initiation site
truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic_duplex.inte: Strain partitioning occurs between gamma and delta; gamma exhibits higher strain hardenability.
truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic_duplex.phas: During aging, kappa' coarsens and may lose coherency; grain boundary kappa* and alpha (B2/DO3) may form.
truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic.phases[aust: initially coherent (K-S OR), loses coherency upon coarsening
truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic.defect_inte: Shearing of kappa' carbides by dislocations enhances planar slip and reduces work hardening rate.
truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic.interface_n: Planar glide dominates; slip bands refine dynamically (DSBR) or form microbands (MBIP) depending on SRO/precipitates.
truth.interfaces.sample_austenitic.phase_evolu: Aging: SRO -> kappa' (L'12) -> kappa (E21); overaging: grain boundary kappa* and b-Mn.

prediction.interfaces.sample_fe18mn10al09c5ni_01.de: Dislocations pile up or bow out at alpha(B2) phase boundaries; B2 particles are not sheared by gliding dislocations

160-175 GPa for Fe-20Mn-(7-10)Al-(0.7-1.2)C cold rolled and annealed; a 2% reduction per 1% Al addition
200 J/cm2 (CVN) at RT in solution-treated condition; much reduced after aging
truth.properties.sample_austenitic.chemical.co: far inferior to 304 stainless steel, comparable to conventional high strength steels

truth.performance.sample_austenitic.comparativ: specific energy absorption ~0.5 J/mm3, similar to Mn TWIP steels and 50% higher than DC04/HSLA steels

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.technique: OM, SEM, TEM, XRD, ECCI, APT
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Microstructural characterization of phases, precipitates, dislocations, and deformation mechanisms.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Revealed SRO, kappa' carbides, planar glide, deformation twins, ordered phases.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_austenit: Assess strength, elongation, strain hardening behaviour.
Yield strength 0.4-1.0 GPa, UTS 0.6-2.0 GPa, elongation 30-100% in austenitic steels; strain hardening dominated by planar glide and SRO/kappa' interaction.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_austenit: Detected austenite and ferrite; kappa-prime not detected by XRD
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe304mn8: Pronounced planar slip and crystallographically aligned slip bands; dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) observed
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_austenit: SRO zones <2 nm observed in austenite after solution treatment and quenching
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe18mn10: B2 stringer bands and nano-sized B2 particles along austenite grain boundaries and shear bands
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_ferritic: Recrystallized delta-ferrite grains ranging from 40 to 90 μm
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn8a: Peak hardness ~340 HB after aging at 550°C for approximately 10 h

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Reasonable agreement but deviations at high Mn and Al; e.g., calculated formation temperatures of kappa and M23C6 underestimated by up to 150 °C.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Estimate density of ferritic and austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys
Composition boundary for intragranular kappa' precipitation
Based on critical lattice constant of austenite ~0.3670 nm.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated phase diagrams compared with experimental isotherms and polythermal sections; reasonable agreement at moderate compositions but deviations noted at high Mn and high Al contents

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Young's modulus of low density steels decreases by 2-2.5% per 1 wt% Al addition and is significantly lower than that of conventional HSLA steels (~160-175 GPa vs 210 GPa).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Addition of Al suppresses cementite precipitation and promotes short-range ordering (SRO) and kappa'-carbide, which leads to glide plane softening and planar slip.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Three new deformation mechanisms are introduced for high-SFE austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels: microband induced plasticity (MBIP), dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR), and shear band induced plasticity (SIP).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Effective Al composition range for austenitic low-density steels with best mechanical properties is 7-10 wt% Al, with typical compositions: 15-30% Mn, 7-10% Al, 0.7-1.2% C.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The ferrite based duplex low-density steels can achieve a combination of TRIP and TWIP effects, showing UTS up to 900 MPa and total elongation up to 70%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Specific stiffness of low density steels (E^(n)/rho) is not higher than that of HSLA steels and aluminium alloys; therefore, parts must be selected where stiffness is not the limiting factor.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fatigue behaviour data are limited and conflicting; formability, weldability, and coatability are not well documented.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Corrosion resistance of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys is not improved over conventional high strength steels; coating is needed for automotive applications.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Specific stiffness (E^n/ρ) of low density steels is not higher than HSLA steels and aluminium alloys; weight saving requires application in parts where stiffness is not the limiting factor.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Young's modulus of cold rolled Fe-20Mn-(7-10)Al-(0.7-1.2)C steels is approximately 20% lower than HSLA grades (~210 GPa).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Stacking fault energy values reported: Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.8C ~85 mJ/m^2; Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C ~120 mJ/m^2; Fe-30.4Mn-8Al-1.2C ~85 mJ/m^2; Fe-27Mn-12Al-0.9C ~110 mJ/m^2; Fe-18Mn-9.6Al-0.65C ~66 mJ/m^2.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Solution-treated austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels exhibit CVN impact energy of ~200 J/cm^2 at room temperature, comparable to austenitic stainless steels; aging at 550°C greatly reduces toughness.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fe-30Mn-10Al-1C-1Si in solution-treated condition shows CVN ~200 J/cm^2 at RT; after aging at 550°C for 16 h impact energy is reduced to almost zero.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fe-28Mn-5Al-1C solutionized at 1050°C for 75 min shows CVN impact energy ~200 J/cm^2 at RT.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Dynamic fracture toughness (J-integral) of solution-treated Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C-xSi is ~71.5 J/cm^2, much higher than age-hardened conditions (9.5-37.5 J/cm^2).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Specific energy absorption of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels with Al>7% is ~0.5 J/mm^3, similar to Mn TWIP steels and about 50% higher than conventional deep drawing steels.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Corrosion resistance of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys is inferior to 304 stainless steel and comparable to conventional high strength steels.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Fatigue behaviour data are conflicting and insufficient for generalised conclusions; some reports show cyclic softening associated with planar slip and shearing of kappa-carbides.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Formability data are limited; hole expansion ratio of austenite based duplex steels is comparable to DP600 but bending angle and dome expansion are lower than conventional AHSS.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Precipitation of intragranular kappa-prime carbide in austenitic steels requires Al >7% and C >0.7%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Glide plane softening due to short-range order or kappa-prime carbides leads to planar slip regardless of high stacking fault energy.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: Deformation mechanisms shift from TRIP/TWIP to MBIP, DSBR, and SIP as Al and C contents increase.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_14.value: Negative work hardening rate is observed in highly alloyed Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.5C and peak-aged Fe-28Mn-12Al-1C, which existing mechanisms cannot fully explain.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_15.value: Industrial processing challenges include Al2O3 nozzle clogging, surface oxide formation, cracking during hot and cold forming, and reduced solidus temperatures due to segregation.
